article_title: Trivy Supply Chain Attack Escalates as TeamPCP Hijacks 75 GitHub Action Tags, Defaced Aqua Security Repositories, and Spreads to npm

content_preview.title: Trivy Supply Chain Attack Escalates as TeamPCP Hijacks 75 GitHub Action Tags, Defaced Aqua Security Repositories, and Spreads to npm
content_preview.summary: Threat actor TeamPCP compromised the widely used Trivy vulnerability scanner through a retained access token from an earlier incomplete remediation, injecting credential-stealing payloads into official releases and GitHub Actions while defacing 44 Aqua Security repositories.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

A multi-stage supply chain attack against Aqua Security's Trivy vulnerability scanner, one of the most widely deployed open-source security tools in DevSecOps, has escalated significantly since it first surfaced on March 19, 2026. The threat actor TeamPCP exploited credentials retained from an earlier, incompletely remediated breach to inject credential-stealing malware into official Trivy releases, hijack dozens of GitHub Action tags, deface 44 Aqua Security repositories, and sprea...

editor_notes.content_quality: Outstanding investigative-style cybersecurity reporting. Comprehensive timeline, detailed attack chain with specific technical indicators, and actionable remediation guidance. Excellent structure covering escalation phases.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 3 sources fetched and thoroughly verified. THN (infostealer article): confirms Docker images 0.69.5/0.69.6, 44-repo defacement with tpcp-docs- prefix, CanisterWorm npm worm, Kubernetes wiper targeting Iranian systems via DaemonSets, CVE-2026-33634 CVSS 9.4. CyberScoop: confirms 1,000+ SaaS environments, Mandiant 10,000 projection, Carmakal quotes verbatim, threat group collaboration US/Canada/UK, 'very loud, very aggressive.' THN (GitHub Actions article): confirms Argon-DevOps-Mgt service account, late February misconfiguration, March 1 incomplete rotation, March 19 17:43 UTC force-push of 75/76 tags, v0.69.4, TeamPCP Cloud stealer memory scraping, AES-256-CBC/RSA-4096 encryption, scan.aquasecurtiy[.]org C2, 45.148.10.212, tpcp-docs fallback, SHA pinning recommendation.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every specific claim verified: CVE number, CVSS score, service account name, timestamps, IP addresses, encryption methods, domain names, version numbers, tag counts, and all quotes. No discrepancies found.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Reports severity without sensationalism. Technical depth appropriate for the target audience.
editor_notes.originality: No prior coverage of the Trivy supply chain attack. Unique and timely topic.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Exemplary cybersecurity news submission with meticulous sourcing and technical accuracy. Every claim cross-verified. Ready for immediate publication.
